intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data indicates a severe discrepancy between the registry and the physical state of the biome. P1 (String of Nickels) is not visible as a living specimen. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct living plants. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by the white rabbit anchor and soil debris.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty soil surface; no signs of succulent growth.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Empty soil surface; no signs of mint growth.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: Contains white rabbit anchor; soil appears undisturbed and devoid of Pothos foliage.
plant_audit.P4_Shared_Black_Pot: No evidence of Silver Guest specimen.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Consistently dark, damp appearance across all pots; no visible fungal blooms or crusting.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant debris or organic matter outside of the pots.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Across the 5-day sequence, there is zero change in the biological state. The pots remain in a state of total systemic loss throughout the observation period.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-functional. All registered specimens are absent or deceased. The environment is essentially a collection of empty pots and a static anchor.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a white rabbit anchor in a pot devoid of its associated Pothos plant is the primary anomaly.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image stack. My first step was to establish the baseline registry and compare it against the visual evidence in each frame. I validated this by cross-referencing the spatial coordinates of each pot across the chronological sequence. The conclusion is definitive: the biome has suffered a total systemic loss of all registered plant life. The pots contain only soil and, in the case of P3, a decorative anchor. There is no evidence of growth, decay, or recovery over the 5-day period.
